issue_title: Problems calling super method of RendererClass
issue_body:
First, thanks for that very useful bindings. I'm having trouble calling the super method of a renderer class.

Let's say that i have an object like this:
````
class CustomPreprocessor(object):
    def preprocess(self, text):
         return text

class CustomRenderer(misaka.HtmlRenderer, CustomPreprocessor):
    def header(self, text, level):
         rendered = super().header(text, level)
         return 'Doesn't make sense %s' % rendered
````
AttributeError: 'super' object has no attribute 'header'

Am I am missing something important? Am I'm overwriting the base header method in HtmlRenderer permanetly?

Thank's a lot!

issue_title: Percent encoding a query parameter whose value contains the equal sign '='
issue_body:
I want to create this query string:
```python
str(f.query) == 'where=1%3D1'
```

Expecting query parameters and values to be decoded automatically, I tried this:
```python
f = furl.furl()
f.args['where'] = '1=1'
```
but got an undesired result:
```python
str(f.query) == 'where=1=1'
```

Alternatively, I tried encoding the parameter value before passing it to the furl object:
```python
f = furl.furl()
f.args['where'] = urllib.quote_plus('1=1')
```
but found the percent encoding encoded again:
```python
str(f.query) == 'where=1%253D1'
```

How can I create the literal query string `where=1%3D1`?

Hi guys, 
Trying to run a `$ntfy send test` command after installing ntfy using `sudo pip install ntfy` and i get the following exception:

```
ERROR: Failed to send notification using default
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ntfy/__init__.py", line 48, in notify
    **backend_config)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/ntfy/backends/default.py", line 22, in notify
    raise DefaultNotifierError(e, module)

DefaultNotifierError
```

I searched something like this in the closed issues and i found the issue #95, where the SteveEdson had the same problem and he said that he ran the command `$ cat ~/.config/ntfy/ntfy.yml` to check if the config file is ok, and there i noticed that this path doesn't exist on my `.config` folder. So I created the ntfy folder using `mkdir ntfy` and then i used a text editor to create the `ntfy.yml` and put the following code inside it:
```
--- 
backends: 
    - linux
```

so, when i ran `$ ntfy send test` again, it resulted in the follow message:

`ImportError: No module named dbus`

So i realized that i don't had the dbus package installed on my machine, then i ran `sudo apt-get install python-dbus` to install this and when i ran again `$ntfy send test` it works pretty nice!

Maybe it is too obvious or a newbie question, but i decide to post it here because it stucked me when i try to run the script for the first time, and i wasted some time to discover how to fix that. I hope that issue can help someone that have the same trouble when try to install the app, so i think that can be usefull.

se ya! :)

issue_title: Stacking tornado_session.Results.close calls doesn't close any connection
issue_body:
Maybe I am doing something wrong, but if I were to simply wait until later in the execution stack to call close on a tornado result, it never closes.

~~~python
conn = queries.queries.TornadoSession(pool_max_size=3)
....
res1 = yield conn.result('some query')
res2 = yield conn.result('some query')
res3 = yield conn.result('some query')

res1.free()
res2.free()
res3.free()

res4 = yield conn.result('some query')
# PoolFullError: Pool <queries.pool.Pool object at $ID> is at its maximum capacity
res4.free()
~~~

If I were to free a result before using the connection again, things work fine. (I even tested with a counter by inspecting the conn_pool_manager._pools dict)

Am I missing something?
Need to test also against timezone aware datetimes, as they're quite popular, especially in Django.

issue_title: New configuration option `paths` not usable from .ini configuration file
issue_body:
We are currently (ab)using the `path` configuration option to define the top-level directory of the source code that should be scanned (best reason is to be more robust in the context of a continuous integration tool). Since the change in commit 7390602899d4a710ad9155f7b728c5901c0d4f74 the value returned by ArgumentParser is probably a list, but ConfigParser will still return a single string.

What's the right solution? Should we go back to skip all possible directories by hand (which is actually the only documented method) or attempt to fix this issue (which is roughly checking if the configuration argument is a string and `split` it)?

Line 69 in __init__.py reads

print "var", var

which fails in Python 3.  A simple fix is:

try:
    print "var", var   # Python 2
except:
    print("var", var)   # Python 3

issue_title: Supervisor can kill the main process, but not the scheduler, web server, and xmpp server
issue_body:
Running Will under supervisor, with the following config:

```
[program:will]
command=/apps/will/venv/bin/python /apps/will/will/run_will.py
process_name=will
autostart=true
stdout_logfile=/var/log/will/bot.log
redirect_stderr=true
priority=500
environment=PYTHONPATH="/apps/will:$PYTHONPATH",
    [lots of other env vars]
```

The problem I'm seeing is that starting Will under supervisor works fine, it starts 4 processes (main and the scheduler,  web server, and xmpp server). But when using `supervisorctl stop will`, it kills the main process but leaves the subprocesses running (and running as the root user, weirdly).

Any tips on how to run will with supervisor properly?
When writing new plugins, the ability to reload would be nice.  Further, my experience with other bots has shown that it's really helpful to the non-programmer to be able to reload a crashed plugin.

issue_title: 'platform' module conflict with python built-in module
issue_body:
```
$ cat a.py
import platform
print platform.uname()[0]
$ python a.py
FreeBSD
$ python -m plop.collector a.py
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/runpy.py", line 162, in _run_module_as_main
    "__main__", fname, loader, pkg_name)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/runpy.py", line 72, in _run_code
    exec code in run_globals
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/plop/collector.py", line 104, in main
    exec f.read() in globals(), globals()
  File "<string>", line 2, in <module>
AttributeError: 'module' object has no attribute 'uname'
```

This is because collector pass globals() to user script, but `__package__='plop'` too.

Bad due to shifting:
`C1*C2/(K*C3) - 1`

better:
`C1*C2 - K*C3`

issue_title: CRLF at the end of the cell gets trimmed
issue_body:
Hi! I've encountered the situation where CRLF at the end of the cell value gets trimmed when output is done as plain text csv (without rendering). With rendering CRLF is kept. Examples:

Kept with rendering:
```
$ daff diff <(echo text) <(echo -e "text\n\"\r\n\"")
@@ ,text
+++,"
"
```
But trimmed without:
```
$ daff --no-color diff <(echo text) <(echo -e "text\n\"\r\n\"")
@@,text
+++,""
```
This occurs in Csv.hx in renderCell function between lines 126-134. \r, \n are first stored into line_buf and later appended to result. But when cell text ends with \r, \n they don't get appended. Is this on purpose?

issue_title: CPU Resources decrease after every connection try/timeout
issue_body:
Running pygatt on a Raspberry Pi B+ (Linux raspberrypi 4.1.13+ #826 PREEMPT Fri Nov 13 20:13:22 GMT 2015 armv6l GNU/Linux).
This piece of code
```
while True:
    # wait for scale to wake up and connect to it
    try:
        device = adapter.connect(ble_address, 5 , 'random')
        break
    except pygatt.exceptions.NotConnectedError:
        time.sleep( 5 )
        pass
```
starts using 2% CPU but this gradually builds to 98%. Connection Timeout of 5s increases, sleep time does not.
```
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 19:39:31 ERROR    connect Timed out connecting to f1:37:57:6c:dc:65 after 5 seconds.
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 19:39:36 INFO     connect Connecting with timeout=5
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 19:39:41 ERROR    connect Timed out connecting to f1:37:57:6c:dc:65 after 5 seconds.
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 19:39:46 INFO     connect Connecting with timeout=5
```
but after a while...

```
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 20:18:13 INFO     connect Connecting with timeout=5
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 20:18:54 ERROR    connect Timed out connecting to f1:37:57:6c:dc:65 after 5 seconds.
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 20:19:00 INFO     connect Connecting with timeout=5
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 20:19:42 ERROR    connect Timed out connecting to f1:37:57:6c:dc:65 after 5 seconds.
Tue, 09 Feb 2016 20:19:47 INFO     connect Connecting with timeout=5
```
The connection timeout has grown to 42 seconds.
It may not seem to be a great idea to try connecting over and over. In this particular case i need to connect to a bathroom scale which only wakes up for one minute after someone steps on it.
Any thoughts?

EDIT: increasing Connection timeout to 60 seconds will have this timeout increase to 76 seconds over one hour of operation.

issue_title: Subtract two curves with evaluate_multi() gives non-zero values
issue_body:
Two curves both alike in domain (0,1) in python interpreter, where we lay our scene...

```python
def curve2d(a):
	interval = 1.0/len(a)
	nodes = []
	step = 0
	for p in a:
		nodes.append([step*interval, p])
		step += 1
	return bezier.Curve.from_nodes(np.array(nodes))

l = np.linspace(0,1, 16)
print curve2d([1,7,4,7,3,5,7,8,8,8]).evaluate_multi(l)- curve2d([5,4,4,4,7,3]).evaluate_multi(l)
[[  0.00000000e+00  -4.00000000e+00]
 [  4.44444444e-03  -1.23661198e+00]
 [  8.88888889e-03   6.29255876e-02]
 [  1.33333333e-02   6.45206528e-01]
 [  1.77777778e-02   8.52927217e-01]
 [  2.22222222e-02   8.74612610e-01]

issue_body: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/29831489/numpy-1-hot-array

The following errors have been found using pyIbex 1.1.3 and are probably derived from IBEX.
1. Interval.ALL_REALS.contains(∞) should be false, because an interval is a subset of the reals and ∞ is no real number (same is true for -∞).
2. pyIbex.atan2([0, 0], [entire]) should contain [0, π], but is [empty].
3. pyIbex.atan2([-2, -0.1], [entire]) should contain [-π, 0], but is [empty].
4. pyIbex.atan2([-2, 0], [entire]) should contain [-π, π], but is [empty].
5. pyIbex.atan2([-2, 0], [-2, 1]) should contain [-π, π], but is [-π, 0].
6. pyIbex.atan2([0, 1], [entire]) should contain [0, π], but is [empty].
7. pyIbex.atan2([0.1, 1], [entire]) should contain [0, π], but is [empty].
8. pyIbex.atanh([1, ∞]) and pyIbex.atanh([1, 1]) return [+∞], which is not an interval, should be [empty].
9. pyIbex.atanh([-∞, -1]) and pyIbex.atanh([-1, -1]) return [-∞], which is not an interval, should be [empty].
10. `pyIbex.sin(Interval(float.fromhex('-0X1.921FB54442D18P+1'), 0.0))` does not cover 0 as a possible result.
11. pyIbex.bwd_abs([-1.9, 0.2], [-0.2, 0.2]) increases the size of x, which should never happen for backward arithmetics.
12. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-1, 5], 0, [-51, 12]) reduces the size of x, which is wrong.
13. pyIbex.bwd_pow([1, 1], 0, [entire]) should produce x = [entire], but is x = [-1, 1].

The following cases of poor accuracy could be improved:
1. pow([empty], 0) returns [1, 1], should be [empty].
2. pyIbex.sqrt([entire]), pyIbex.sqrt([0, 1]) and other values for x produce a negative lower boundary, should be zero.
3. `pyIbex.tan(Interval(0.0, float.fromhex('0X1.921FB54442D18P+0')))` returns [entire], should be [0, 1.63312e+16].
4. `pyIbex.bwd_abs(Interval(float.fromhex('-0x1p-1022'), float.fromhex('-0x1p-1022')), Interval.ALL_REALS)` should produce x = [empty]. **Also this function call modifies the content of Interval.ALL_REALS, which is bad.**
5. pyIbex.bwd_abs([-1, 0], [entire]) should produce x = [0, 0], but is [-1, 1].
6. pyIbex.bwd_abs([-∞, 0], [entire]) should produce x = [0, 0], but is [entire].
7. pyIbex.bwd_abs([-∞, -1], [entire]) should produce x = [empty], but is [entire].
8. pyIbex.bwd_abs([-∞, 1], [entire]) should produce x = [-1, 1], but is [entire].
9. Interval(float.fromhex('-0X0.0000000000002P-1022'), float.fromhex('0X0.0000000000001P-1022')).mid() should use IEEE 754 rounding mode “to nearest, ties to even” and produce 0.
10. pyIbex.bwd_cosh([empty], [0, ∞]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [0, ∞].
11. pyIbex.bwd_cosh([empty], [entire]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [entire].
12. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-1, 0], 0, [-1, 1]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [-1, 1].
13. pyIbex.bwd_pow([0, 0], -1, [-5.1, 55.5]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [0, 0].
14. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-∞, 0], -3, [5.1, 55.5]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [5.1, 55.5].
15. pyIbex.bwd_pow([0, ∞], 3, [entire]) should not produce a negative lower boundary.
16. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-∞, 0], 3, [entire]) should not produce a positive upper boundary.
17. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-10, 0], -2, [entire]) should produce x = [empty], but is x = [entire].
18. pyIbex.bwd_pow([-∞, 0], -7, [entire]) should produce x = [-∞, 0], but is [entire].
There are perhaps too many and they could be better organized. Filter to what is actually needed. Dump any non-working badges, etc.

Consider this simple program:

```
x = 50
while True:
    pass
```

If you set a breakpoint on the very first line, start the debugger, it stops at the breakpoint as expected. If you then hit Resume then Suspend, it resumes and stops again at some point in the loop as expected, but if you hit resume then suspend again, it resumes but never suspends again unless there's a breakpoint inside the loop. I've been trying to track this down but I couldn't figure out why this happens. Any thoughts?

Thank you!

issue_title: bug: is_match() fails when comparing empty iterables
issue_body: The `is_match()` function should return ``True`` if two iterables are the same type and are empty.

As reported by harti:

> I observed this by chance when encoding `32768`. The current code yields `0x8000`. Since integers are signed (also the unsigned types!) this actually is a -0. The correct encoding is `0x008000`. The following code in `Integer` seems to fix this, but it deserves probably more testing for the edge cases:
>  
>     def __bytes__(self):
>         if self.value == 0:
>             octets = [0]
>         else:
>             # Split long integers into multiple octets.
>             remainder = self.value
>             octets = []
>            
>             while True:
>                 octets.append(remainder & 0b11111111)
>                 if remainder == 0 or remainder == -1:
>                     break
>                 remainder = remainder >> 8
>             if remainder == 0 and octets[-1] == 0b10000000:
>                 octets.append(0)
>             octets.reverse()
>            
>             # remove leading octet if there is a string of 9 zeros or ones
>             while len(octets) > 1 and (octets[0] == 0 and octets[1] & 0b10000000 == 0) or \
>                 (octets[0] == 0b11111111 and octets[1] & 0b10000000 != 0):
>                 del octets[0]
>                
>         tinfo = TypeInfo(self.TYPECLASS, TypeInfo.PRIMITIVE, self.TAG)
>         return bytes(tinfo) + bytes([len(octets)]) + bytes(octets)

issue_title: vcrpy breaks when using an authenticated proxy for an HTTPS request
issue_body:
I'm trying to generate some tests cases for requests that need to use an http proxy and I get an HTTP 407 from the proxy server.

After inspecting what's being sent when using vcrpy I found that it doesn't perform the `CONNECT` to start tunneling to the https site. It appears to send some binary data instead. I assume it's skipping the process of performing the initial setup of the proxy request and sending the proxy authentication.

How to replicate:
1. Run netcat -l -p 4444
2. Run the following script
```
import vcr
import requests
import logging

with vcr.use_cassette('headers.yml'):
    requests.get('https://httpbin.org/ip', proxies={"https": "http://user:pass@localhost:4444"})
```
3. In netcat's output you'll see that there's no `CONNECT` command or proxy authorization 

If you run the same request without using vcrpy the `CONNECT` and proxy authorization are sent correctly.

issue_title: Add/Parse Information about Secondary Structure Elements
issue_body:
Add a new dataframe object as `PandasPdb.df['SSE']`, which contains secondary structure element information. This pandas `DataFrame` would have the same number of rows as the coordinate section DataFrame (`'ATOM'`) and columns "helix" and "sheet" with type "bool." 

For ease of use, the SSE dataframe could share the dataframe indices with the `PandasPdb.df['ATOM']` section. It may only get tricky if one ('ATOM' or 'SSE') gets updated & reindex and not the other.

issue_title: cache files are deprecated in each pywal release
issue_body:
Hi @dylanaraps I was taking a look at the new naming convention for the cache_file for each image, I noticed that with this way of differentiating the files, the users will lost all changes in a color-scheme with each new release of `pywal`, as they will be ignored and a new one will be generated, I have two suggestions that might help avoid this issue.

* handle cache file versions separately from `pywal`'s version,
so instead of appending `pywal.__version__` you could append something like `pywal.__cache_version__`, which would only change each time there's a breaking change in the way you do cache files.

* be able to specify a custom cache_file name to `color.get()` to allow `wpgtk` or other software that relies on `pywal` to decide it's own naming convention

I personally believe that my first suggestion is the best of the two, it's simpler and will please your users too (not loosing their color-schemes on each update).

I've done a PR #177 to implement this, please let me know your thoughts on this matter.

anyway, thanks for reading!

issue_title: Incorrect Parsing of Floating Point Literals
issue_body:
Currently, the floating point literal
```
double f = 4.5;
```
is parsed to 
```
Decl: f, [], [], []
  TypeDecl: f, []
    IdentifierType: ['double']
  Constant: float, 4.5
```
when really, the `type` attribute of the `Constant` node should be "double". Likewise, 
```
double f = 4.5L;
```
is parsed to 
```
Decl: f, [], [], []
  TypeDecl: f, []
    IdentifierType: ['double']
  Constant: float, 4.5L
```
when the `type` attribute of the `Constant` node should be "long double".  Note that the declared type is not what matters, it is the parsing of the floating point literal.

I'm trying to use IPDB in 'direct' mode which as far as I understand should cause any commands to apply immediately without the need for commit()?

```
ipdb = IPDB(mode='direct')

If I then run the 'ip link' command I don't see the veth interfaces on my system. If I then commit in the following way:

ipdb.interfaces.v0p0.commit()

issue_title: RTM_GETNSID fails; generates message of size 25 instead of 28
issue_body:
I'm trying to do a `IPRoute().nlm_request(req, RTM_GETNSID)`. I'm using pyroute2 0.5.0.

```python
from pyroute2 import IPRoute
import socket
from pyroute2.netlink import NLM_F_REQUEST
import pyroute2.netlink.rtnl as rtnl
import pyroute2.netlink.rtnl.nsidmsg as nsidmsg

netstack = IPRoute()
with open('/proc/{}/ns/net'.format(12345), "r") as netnsf: # PID 12345
    req = nsidmsg.nsidmsg()
    req['rtgen_family'] = 0
    req['attrs'] = [('NETNSA_FD', netnsf.fileno())]
    req.encode()
    ret = netstack.nlm_request(req, rtnl.RTM_GETNSID, NLM_F_REQUEST)
```

The request fails with error 22, invalid argument. Strace shows this, indicating that only 25 octets are indicated in the header:
```
sendto(5, {{len=25, type=0x5a /* NLMSG_??? */, flags=NLM_F_REQUEST, seq=255, pid=31204}, "\x00\x00\x00\x00\x08\x00\x03\x00\x06"}, 28, 0, {sa_family=AF_NETLINK, nl_pid=0, nl_groups=00000000}, 12) = 28
```

As the FD should be encoded as uint32, I would expect at least three more octets for the FD.

Comparing this to a RTM_GETNSID request from `lsns` I see that the request should have a length of 28.

issue_title: Addic7ed is not getting the subtitles for some series
issue_body:
Hi,
First, thanks for this beautiful tool.
I am using subliminal lib from last version of sickrage (https://github.com/SickRage/SickRage). I compared addic7ed.py and file from my sickrage is the same that the one on subliminal master repo.
I configured it to use my account on addic7ed site and my profile on this site is configured to display subtitles of all languages.
It works pretty well except for 2 series where it gets nothing for both my languages (FR, EN) while there are files on addic7ed for the languages with same release group:
- The 100 - id=268592
- Scandal (2012) - id=248841

Log shows it didn't find any file:
```

issue_title: CompressedStaticFilesMixin compresses each CSS file multiple times
issue_body:
I noticed as part of #148, that after modifying `CompressedStaticFilesMixin` so that it passes `quiet=False` to `Compressor`, some files are run through the compressor multiple times:
```
$ ./manage.py collectstatic --noinput -v 3 > collectstatic.log
$ grep -e 'Brotli compressed .*\.css ' collectstatic.log | sort
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.31652d31b392.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.6b517d0d5813.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.6b517d0d5813.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/base.css (15K -> 3K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.css (6K -> 1K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.css (6K -> 1K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.css (6K -> 1K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.f6dc691f8d62.css (6K -> 1K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.f6dc691f8d62.css (6K -> 1K)
Brotli compressed /home/vagrant/test/test/static/admin/css/changelists.f6dc691f8d62.css (6K -> 1K)
...
```

This is due to a bug in Django's `HashedFilesMixin`, for which I've filed an upstream issue:
https://code.djangoproject.com/ticket/28607

It only affects "adjustable files" (ie files that `HashedFilesMixin` has to do a second pass on, in case they cross-reference other files that have been hashed, so might need renaming with a new hash) - which currently is just *.css. However it still has a significant impact on the number of compressions performed:
```
$ grep 'Brotli compressed' collectstatic.log | wc -l
370
$ grep 'Brotli compressed' collectstatic.log | sort -u | wc -l
276
```

issue_title: Add type for Markdown with wrapping
Report PASS for PASS in Filter column instead of empty list
issue_body:
See Issue #153 which was closed by the reporter after a developer suggested that existing behavior (which is to return an empty list for FILTER, when the filter column contains "PASS".

I was surprised by this behavior, instead expecting parser to pass through what was in the VCF file -- returning an empty list for '.' but reporting a string if a string is there).  I wanted to suggest again that this behavior be changed to report "PASS" for the following reason.

This point is subtle but important: *PASS carries semantic meaning* and should be passed through -- **the VCF specification(1) allows an empty string in the FILTER column to signify that filters have not been applied**. This makes "PASS" distinct from the empty string. 

Current behavior does not conform to the VCF specification, in that it does not differentiate between the empty value and PASS. I therefore recommend that ["PASS"] should be returned, instead of the empty list.   I am happy to issue a pull request if extra hands are needed for this.

(1) VCF 4.1 specs page 4, item#7

issue_title: Support returning an iterator (at least for DENSE and LOD)
issue_body: To allow liac-arff to be memory efficient, but not commit to a particular in-memory representation or libraries, it could have an option which returned the data as an iterator/generator (using `return_type`, or as well as it?). It could either generate one row at a time, or use a [pandas-style `chunksize`](https://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/generated/pandas.read_csv.html) to deliver multiple rows at a time. Either way it could be used, together with the returned metadata about attributes, to store data in a compact or out-of-memory format on the fly (e.g. using [`numpy.fromiter`](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy-1.13.0/reference/generated/numpy.fromiter.html)).

issue_title: "This field must be unique" error when trying to update device owned by multiple users
issue_body:
In my application, I support multiple users using the same device (and thus having the same registration ID). Create and delete queries work fine this way (the registration ID correctly gets set to inactive for all other users when it's created for one user). But when trying to update an existing registration ID, to make it inactive when the user logs out from the device, I get a "This field must be unique" error on the registration_id field.

Expected behaviour: if the update sets the notification device to active, set notification devices with the same registration ID owned by other users to inactive and then do the update. Otherwise, just do the update.

Cause: in api/rest_framework.py, there's logic to handle this for create queries, starting at line 42. The same logic is missing for update queries (starting at line 39).

Workaround: delete and re-create the notification device instead.

issue_title: deep learning training speed up- only perform feature scaling once
issue_body:
right now the whole pipeline runs for each fold of the cv. this is incredibly inefficient. for deep learning in particular, where we need to try many different architectures, the training of the net itself isn't too computationally expensive, but running the feature scaling part a billion times is. 

sure, we can definitely look into speeding up feature scaling. but, we should also look at just performing the setup part of the pipeline once (cleaning values, doing all the transformation, and dataframevectorizing them) just a single time, then pass those values into a simpler GSCV that just does GSCV on the final model (where the final model is, of course, an instance of FinalModelATC). then we'll simply append that trained/tuned model to the end of our pipeline.

issue_title: Ubuntu Package lists PyQt4 and xclip as dependencies.
issue_body:
Not directly a pyperclip issue, but the pyperclip Ubuntu package declares PyQt4 and xclip as a dependency. As a consequence, all packages that depend on pyperclip transitively depend on libqt4, libx11, etc.. We had a few mitmproxy users who were not so amused by this (especially if you install it on Ubuntu Server...), and I'd love to start a discussion how this could be tackled. As I see it, pyperclip should list none of those as a dependency.

http://packages.ubuntu.com/de/xenial/python-pyperclip

cc @sdelafond

Most python deployments have TKinter. This should be way more portable than usage of gtk / qt / xclip and xsel as well a much faster than popen

```python
try:
    from Tkinter import Tk
except ImportError:
    from tkinter import Tk

def clip_set(text):
     r=Tk()
     r.withdraw()
     r.clipboard_clear()
     r.clipboard_append(text)
     r.destroy()


def clip_get():
     r=Tk()
     r.withdraw()
     ttt = r.clipboard_get()
     r.destroy()
     return ttt
```

-------------------------

If you lose connection the file download will pause and resume when reconnected. This is good.

If you interrupt the download (kill the script) you can end up with broken files that bandcamp-dl will then skip on next execution because it does not check if they actually completed, just that they exist. This is bad.

Testing a Google Fiber 1G connection, I was seeing higher download speeds than before, but upload speeds of around 200M instead of the over 800 speed I've seen before.  I tested using two different hosts, different versions of Debian, different connections to the local net, different versions of the client (Debian native and current GitHub.)  On the basis of these tests, I successfully engaged the support technicians at Google, and got very cooperative responses.  As a last step to make sure the ticket would not be pushed back when it was escalated to the engineers, I did an official web-based test using Google's own speed test (http://speedtest.googlefiber.net/).  That test showed close to 900M upload speeds.

Google's speed test has not been shown to be biased.  I had been using the local Google Fiber speedtest host for the cli tests.

This is really embarassing.  I realize that not many developers can afford a symmetrical link, let alone a gigabit, but I think there may be serious bias in the test toward asymmetrical connections and a lower expectation for uploads that precludes accuracy.  

There are not many alternatives to this program.  It appears to fill a niche without much competition.   As such, a higher attention to accuracy should be expected.

I am happy to offer my services testing the client on a symetrical gigabit connection if someone can identify the problem and update the code.

issue_title: Transactions don't work with empty values.
issue_body:
At the moment, python-etcd3 code for transactions doesn't allow no value to be defined in compare operations (which would be, in SQL terms, the NULL term).

This means that it's impossible to write even simple constructs like "Create this key if it doesn't exist".

```
c = etcd3.client()
res = c.transaction(compare=[c.transactions.create('test') == 0], success=[c.transactions.put('test', 'testvalue')], failure=[]) # This returns false
res = c.transaction(compare=[c.transactions.create('test') == None], success=[c.transactions.put('test', 'testvalue')], failure=[]) # This raises an exception
```

issue_title: Using the Django 2.0 path function for routes results in backslash escaped paths in OpenAPI output
issue_body:
* DRF OpenAPI version: 1.1.0
* Python version: 3.6.2
* Operating System: macOS 10.13.1

### Description

I updated some Django 1.x code to Django 2.0 and changed a number of `url` routes to the new-style `path` function ( https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/ref/urls/#path ). The Swagger HTML output and OpenAPI JSON (`?format=openapi`) output now contains 'backslash escaped' forward slashes, like `/v2.0\/schema\/` instead of the expected `/v2.0/schema/`.

### What I Did

You can reproduce the issue from the `drf_openapi/example` by changing urls.py to:

```
from django.urls import path, re_path
from django.conf.urls import include
from django.contrib import admin

from examples.views import MySchemaView

API_PREFIX = r'^(?P<version>[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)'

urlpatterns = [
    re_path(r'^admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path(f'<str:version>/schema/', MySchemaView.as_view(), name='api_schema'),
    re_path(f'{API_PREFIX}/snippets/', include('snippets.urls')),
]
```
This uses the new Django 2.x `path` function for routes as an example.

![drf _openapi_schema_path_slashes_bug](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/39485/34089131-34350526-e402-11e7-8b69-774fbcb24d21.png)

issue_title: 内容中emoji表情的一种处理方法
issue_body: 有人的昵称中有很多emoji符号，在群聊回复时，每个emoji符号会变成这样的字符串： `""` 穿插在内容中接收端无法正常显示。 其实emoji1f639就是'\u1f639'的unicode编码，但是试了好几种方法都没有正常让接收端显示成功，只好暂时放弃了，用了一个很笨的方法先把这些emoji符号暂时全部过滤掉，实现方法如下： 在clint.py文件中，导入BeautifulSoup模块 `from bs4 import BeautifulSoup` 修改send_msg函数，将'Content'赋值修改为如下方式： `'Content': BeautifulSoup(msg,"lxml").get_text(),` 目前功能使用正常，但是没有检查数据库中用户昵称的一致性问题。

I'm experimenting with machine learning and for the purpose I would need to feed the model with the board position evaluation.

From [engine communication documentation](https://python-chess.readthedocs.io/en/latest/uci.html) seems the output received from the engine is the best and ponder moves, while I would need the value of the evaluation.

Even better would be to be able to have the evaluation of all the possible legal moves (or a subset of it) at once (e.g. stockfish with MultiPV option can give them as you want). Obviously in this case the current position evaluation is the score of the best move.

The EPD spec allows the 'PV' tag (principal variation) to contain a list of moves, which could be executed in order.  the Board.set_epd() function fails to do this because it assums there is only one move, and also assumes that the move is to be played in the current position.

Dumping a bitarray directly via marshal does not store the correct format:

  ```python
  >>> from bitarray import bitarray
  >>> b = bitarray('10001101')
  >>> b
  bitarray('10001101')
  >>> import marshal
  >>> s = marshal.dumps(b)
  >>> c = marshal.loads(s)
  >>> c
  '\x8d'
  >>> b # for comparison
  bitarray('10001101')
  ```

A workaround is to dump and load mytes:

  ```python
  >>> s = marshal.dumps(b.tobytes())
  >>> c = bitarray()
  >>> c.frombytes(marshal.loads(s))
  >>> c
  bitarray('10001101')
  >>> b # for comparison
  bitarray('10001101')
  ```
It would be more convenient when marshaling bitarrays stored in lists, dicts, etc if it was not necessary to do this conversion.

The current validation for IE VAT numbers does not accept this format: IE1234567XX

For instance IE3286438VH is not accepted, even though it's a valid VAT number.
See https://www.gov.uk/guidance/vat-eu-country-codes-vat-numbers-and-vat-in-other-languages

I hate to drop that but I couldn't get any traction otherwise:
I found an OS command injection back in June and I contributed a fix in [PR 114](https://github.com/deanmalmgren/textract/pull/114)

Parsing a file with a malicious name leads to arbitrary OS command injection, this is especially risky when parsing user-supplied files on a server (e.g. uploaded files)

``python
import textract
import sys
import os

\# create a file with a malicious name an arbitrary content
fileName = './test";gnome-calculator;#.pdf'
file = open(fileName,'w+')
file.write('Pierre Ernst, Salesforce')
file.close()

\# parse newly created file
text = textract.process(fileName)
print text

\# cleanup
os.remove(fileName);
``

issue_title: Custom XML entities are coming out as None, as of 0.11
issue_body:
I use xmltodict to parse a rather complex XML file, the "JMdict" Japanese dictionary. The file defines a large number of custom entities, as shorthand for categories such as parts of speech.

I was previously using xmltodict 0.10.2 to parse the file, and it worked correctly. But now, the entities are coming out with values of None.

To reproduce:

- Download JMdict from http://ftp.monash.edu.au/pub/nihongo/JMdict.gz
- Extract the file and rename its extracted content to "JMdict.xml"
- Run this Python code (I'm running it in an IPython 5.1 shell on Python 3.5.3):

```python
>>> import xmltodict
>>> data = open('JMdict.xml').read()
>>> xml = xmltodict.parse(data)
>>> entry = xml['JMdict']['entry'][0]
>>> entry

OrderedDict([('ent_seq', '1000000'),
             ('r_ele',
              [OrderedDict([('reb', 'ヽ')]), OrderedDict([('reb', 'くりかえし')])]),
             ('sense',
              OrderedDict([('pos', None),
                           ('gloss',
                            [OrderedDict([('@xml:lang', 'eng'),
                                          ('#text',
                                           'repetition mark in katakana')]),
                             OrderedDict([('@xml:lang', 'ita'),
                                          ('#text',
                                           'simbolo di ripetizione in katakana')])])]))])
```

The entry being parsed looks like this:

```xml
<entry>
<ent_seq>1000000</ent_seq>
<r_ele>
<reb>ヽ</reb>
</r_ele>
<r_ele>
<reb>くりかえし</reb>
</r_ele>
<sense>
<pos>&n;</pos>
<gloss>repetition mark in katakana</gloss>
<gloss xml:lang="ger">(n) Wiederholung</gloss>
<gloss xml:lang="ger">Refrain</gloss>
</sense>
</entry>
```

Note that the value `&n;` came out as `None`. It used to come out as the string "noun (common) (futsuumeishi)", because line 306 contains:

```xml
<!ENTITY n "noun (common) (futsuumeishi)">
```

I'm working around this for now by downgrading to 0.10.2.
There is a branch based on patch I received (https://github.com/miracle2k/onkyo-eiscp/tree/py3), but we need to support both versions in the same codebase.

Is there a changelog somewhere indicating the differences between versions?  I'd like to know the features/bug fixes/changes different between 1.5 and 1.6.   If there isn't one, I think it would be helpful to include.

[buildout]
parts = python_section pydev_section django_section ipython_section

Dear Mr. Farrell,

I very much appreciate the time and effort you put into this project!
I'm glad that I found your project because otherwise I'd have to write my own table-widget for pandas.DataFrame.

After #47 was fixed, I found out that the consumed memory still grows when table data is updated via updateModel.
While looking at the code, I realized that parts of the UI are newly generated on each call of Table.show (which is called by updateModel) without "destroying" all old widgets properly.
To fix this, some bigger changes are required - I think.

This quick fix unluckily produces an error in some cases:

```diff
--- a/pandastable/core.py
+++ b/pandastable/core.py
@@ -2868,11 +2868,9 @@ class Table(Canvas):
         self.cols = self.model.getColumnCount()
         self.tablewidth = (self.cellwidth)*self.cols
         if hasattr(self, 'tablecolheader'):
-            self.tablecolheader.destroy()
-            self.rowheader.destroy()
             self.selectNone()
-        self.show()
-        return
+        else:
+            self.show()

     def new(self):
         """Clears all the data and makes a new table"""

```
Running my test program from below gives me following values for the current core.py:

	38.30 cycles/second
	23.59 kB/cycle
	18.15 MB gained

After applying the patch from above:

	58.35 cycles/second
	0.52 kB/cycle
	0.63 MB gained

Obviously the patch has also an effect on the performance. #31 

test program:
```python
import psutil
import tkinter as tk
import pandas
import pandastable

DURATION = 20  # seconds the program should run


def update_model():
    df = pandas.DataFrame(list(range(N, N+10)))
    table.updateModel(pandastable.TableModel(df))


def update_title(set_smu=False):
    global N, S
    if set_smu:
        global smu
        smu = me.memory_full_info().uss
    mem = me.memory_full_info().uss
    update_model()
    table.redraw()
    diff = me.memory_full_info().uss - mem
    S += diff
    N += 1
    root.title('total: {:.2f}MB - added per redraw: {:.2f}kB'
               .format(mem / 1000**2, (S/N)/1000))
    root.after(10, update_title)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    N = 0
    S = 0
    me = psutil.Process()
    root = tk.Tk()
    frame = tk.Frame(root)
    table = pandastable.Table(frame)
    table.show()
    frame.pack(fill='both', expand=True)
    root.after(1000, update_title, True)
    update_model()
    root.update()
    root.after(1000 + DURATION*1000, root.destroy)
    root.mainloop()
    print('{:.2f} cycles/second'.format(N/DURATION))
    print('{:.2f} kB/cycle'.format((S/N)/1000))
    print('{:.2f} MB gained'.format((me.memory_full_info().uss-smu)/1000**2))
```

I've tried regenerating the AWS keys, I ensured that the process has the env vars set with the key/secret, and --status-check reports all fine. Nevertheless, my uploads always get Broken Pipe.

This may be a transient error from S3, but it happened to me several times also in the past; anyone has an idea on how to solve this? My solution until now has always been to use the web upload.

I'm susrprised because the connection is dropped after almost 2MB tranferred.

The link where you pointed to rope-like text parsing is from this editor:
https://github.com/martanne/vis

It has all the features in that long mail you linked to in another issue and is written in pure modern C.

`latexcodec` incorrectly escapes ampersands. Consider a string with latex directives like this
```
import codecs
import latexcodec

string = '\\& \\"a \\"o \\"u'
latex = codecs.encode(string, 'latex')
for byte in latex:
    print('{:>3}: {:>2}'.format(byte, codecs.decode(bytes([byte]), 'utf8')))
```
This will output
```
 92:  \
 92:  \
 38:  &
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
 97:  a
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
111:  o
 32:
 92:  \
 34:  "
117:  u
```
As can be seen, while the backslashes for `äüö`  are not duplicated, `latexenc` does not seem to realise that `\\&` is an escape sequence and thus prefixes `&` with another backslash, which need to be escaped in the python string as well.

@contextlib.contextmanager
def set_default_encoding(encoding='ascii'):
    """\

    import sys

issue_title: Make QuerySetMixin capable of wrapping an existing QuerySet
issue_body:
It would be nice to be able to use the extensions with models defined in other apps, without much hackery. For example I want to do something like:

```py
from django.contrib.admin.models import LogEntry
qs = LogEntry.objects.filter(user=x)
qs = QuerySetMixin.wrap(qs).force_index('bla')
```

N.B. old versions of Django used to make this a bit easier in an undocumented fashion - `_clone` took a `klass` parameter that allowed mutation, so a dynamic subclass could be generated. We could do something similar. However wrapped `QuerySet`s are at a risk of being un-pickle-able if the class is purely dynamic, I know this needs some extra stuff to make it work with pickle.

Summary: Subprocess imports throw an error on some architectures

Django Version: Django 1.11.1
Database and version used: CloudSQL MySQL 5.7
Version: Django-MySQL 2.1.0
Python version: 2.7 (GoogleAppEngine)

When deploying on certain cloud architectures (here Google App Engine), the valid way to use subprocess is to do a global import:

`import subprocess`

And then later utilize the imported namespace:

`subprocess.PIPE`

I am happy to send a patch if this is useful for you. For now I have modified my deployed code as a workaround. Thanks for creating a useful library!

#### Description


The ETA looks to be a countdown, not the actual time.

This might be considered pedantic.
ETE is the time remaining, ETA is the time and date an event will happen.
Arguably, ETA has been used in a countdown fashion numerous times before.
But can be really confusing with long progress items,

Reading 0x60 of 0x6000, 3.906%,  ETE: 1 day, 0:00:03,  ETA: Tuesday September 06 10:01:10 PM

e.g.  A flight from SFO to LAX takes 1 hour and 27 minutes.
The remaining flight time will count down from 01:27
If the flight leaves at Noon, it will land at arrive at 1:27pm

ETE: Estimated Time En-route 
ETA: Estimated Time of Arrival

issue_title: Implement automatic translation from pytz to Windows timezone names
issue_body:
The `PYTZ_TO_MS_MAP` hack in `exchangelib.ewsdatetime` is used to translate a Python (pytz) timezone name to a timezone name as expected by EWS, but it is hopelessly incomplete.

We should re-use the translations provided by CLDR, either by contributing an implementation to the `l18n` package (see issue https://bitbucket.org/tkhyn/l18n/issues/3/implement-pytz-to-windows-tz-translation) or by consuming the database directly: http://unicode.org/repos/cldr/trunk/common/supplemental/windowsZones.xml

`MS_TIMEZONE_DEFINITIONS` should either disappear or be replaced by a lazy `services.GetServerTimeZones` call, if `StartTimeZone` et al require a `Name` attribute.

Hi, 

I have a problem regarding timezones. After almost two months of searching I think I can narrow it down to these two error messages:

```
INFO:exchangelib.fields:Encountered naive datetime 2017-12-06 21:10:44 on field last_modified_time. Assuming timezone Europe/Vienna
WARNING:exchangelib.fields:Cannot convert value 'Customized Time Zone' on field '_start_timezone' to type <class 'exchangelib.ewsdatetime.EWSTimeZone'> (unknown timezone ID)
WARNING:exchangelib.fields:Cannot convert value 'Customized Time Zone' on field '_end_timezone' to type <class 'exchangelib.ewsdatetime.EWSDTimeZone'> (unknown timezone ID)
```

My Problem now:
- I create an event (works just fine, timezone correct and all)
- I change the body of the event (nothing else changes) and somehow the timezone gets screwed up.

Is there a way to "add" my timezone ID so that it can be correctly mapped to Europe/Vienna?

issue_title: Python amalgamation script creates a roaring.cpp file whereas original (bash) amalgamation script creates a roaring.c file
issue_body:
CRoaring is a C library. If you take the amalgamated files with our bash script, you can build a working Visual Studio project with them...

https://github.com/lemire/croaringfun

However, it looks like you replaced the bash script with a Python script... nothing wrong with that per se... except that the Python script generates a roaring.cpp file, not a roaring.c file.

Though C is almost a subset of C++, that's not quite true. By using the cpp extension, you are hinting that CRoaring is C++ code, and that's not quite correct.

So I suggest that the Python amalgamation script be made to produce a roaring.c file, instead of a roaring.cpp file. This should ease support for Visual Studio.

issue_title: Wagtail tests failing when using django-storages (S3BotoStorage)
issue_body:
Related
https://github.com/torchbox/wagtail/issues/1542

#### result
```
python runtests.py wagtail.wagtailimages --failfast

======================================================================
FAIL: test_closes_image (wagtail.wagtailimages.tests.test_models.TestGetWillowImage)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/tmp/wagtail-1.0/wagtail/wagtailimages/tests/test_models.py", line 239, in test_closes_image
    self.assertTrue(self.image.file.closed)
AssertionError: False is not true

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 4 tests in 2.563s

FAILED (failures=1)
Destroying test database for alias 'default'...
```

Got  a reply from a wagtail core developer.
I wonder what can be done to fix this issue.

```
This looks like a bug in django-storages to me.

When you call file.close() the file.closed property should return True.

django-storages doesn't override djangos default definition of this property: https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/core/files/base.py#L67-L69

This tells if the file is closed by simply accessing the .file attribute. But in django-storages, this reopens the file: https://github.com/jschneier/django-storages/blob/master/storages/backends/s3boto.py#L120-L138

So .closed returns True
```

issue_title: Amazon S3: can't get file chunk without loading whole file into memory
issue_body:
Example:

    DEFAULT_FILE_STORAGE = "storages.backends.s3boto3.S3Boto3Storage"

    from django.core.files.storage import default_storage
    default_storage.open("mykey").read(1024)

causes MemoryError to me, because it try to load ~5GB file.

It's because in a `S3Boto3StorageFile._get_file` method the whole file is loaded into memory every time we request it:

      ...
      self._is_dirty = False
      self._file.write(self.obj.get()['Body'].read())
      self._file.seek(0)

But boto3 supports reading file by chunk:

    s3.Object('mybucket', 'mykey').get()['Body'].read(1024)

issue_title: S3BotoStorage serializes bucket_name kwarg in Django 1.7
issue_body:
This storage kwarg maybe be a settings variable that changes from deployment to deployment, but the value gets serialized into migrations, corrupting my deployment-agnostic migrations with deployment-specific configuration. 

The bucket_name isn't used during schema migrations, however it could be used during a data migration that affects the given S3BotoStorage FileField. Because of that we can't simply remove the bucket_name from the deconstructed / serialized arguments. 

Instead I propose modifying S3BotoStorage so that it uses a new bucket_alias kwarg instead of bucket_name, where the value of bucket_alias is a key in a settings dictionary of arbitrary keywords to actual S3 bucket names. This indirection will allow us to serialize bucket definitions in migrations but still independently define the exact buckets used for a given deployment.

This type of issue and solution are the reason databases have aliases in Django.

Is there a way to disable it? Or am I missing something.

issue_title: Allow setting a maximum freshness duration
issue_body:
Session cookies used to get deleted when closing the browser. However, with "session restore" features being common among all browsers now usually they do not throw away cookies for tabs that were open when closing the browser. This can result in "session" cookies actually living longer than normal cookies with a predefined TTL.

Depending on how you use freshness this might keep a session fresh for way too long.

---

Another solution instead of implementing a freshness duration would be allowing one to override the `login_fresh()` check (and probably also marking a session/login as fresh) without monkeypatching...

The login out removes the session cookies, but if it's captured before removal, it can be used to access the service as authenticated user.

To recreate:
* Login
* Capture cookie through a browser plugin or a man in the middle proxy
* Logout
* Send a request with captured cookie
* Able to access service as an authenticated user

I found that with `SECURITY_TRACKABLE` enabled, `flask-login.login_fresh()` could identify if the session is stale which checks the User-Agent, IP address etc.
But if the request is coming from the same IP address and same User-Agent,  flask-login still doesn't identify it as stale.

issue_title: Invalid SQL generated for select_for_update when there are joined tables
issue_body:
We have in our project a few `select_for_update()` queries which fail with the error `Incorrect syntax near the keyword 'with'`.

After a bit of investigation, we remarked that the generated queries have the form:

``` sql
SELECT [app_model].[id]
FROM [app_model]
INNER JOIN [app_othermodel] ON ( [app_model].[othermodel_id] = [app_othermodel].[id] )
WITH (ROWLOCK, UPDLOCK)
```

It looks like the expected syntax is instead:

``` sql
SELECT [app_model].[id]
FROM [app_model]
WITH (ROWLOCK, UPDLOCK)
INNER JOIN [app_othermodel] ON ( [app_model].[othermodel_id] = [app_othermodel].[id] )
```

That is, the `WITH` option must directly follow the table name in the `FROM` clause and not the `INNER JOIN` clause.

This was found with django 1.8.13, django-pyodbc-azure 1.8.13.0 and MS SQL Server 2014. We did not test with other SQL Server versions, but we think that the issue applies to all supported versions.

issue_title: Variable definitions not detected without spaces
issue_body:
Variable definitions are not detected when no space is present between the variable type and colon separators. Reported in [ide-fortran issue 8](https://github.com/hansec/ide-fortran/issues/8). Examples below.

```fortran
integer(4):: var1    ! Detected correctly
integer :: var2      ! Detected correctly
integer:: var3       ! Not detected
```

issue_title: Driver provides SSL context to ws:// URI scheme
issue_body:
Connecting to an HTTP endpoint correctly sets `ws://` but incorrectly provides `ssl=context` with `context` being set on line 26 of `websocket.py`
```
context = ssl.create_default_context(purpose=ssl.Purpose.CLIENT_AUTH)
```

This is used on line 39, even if the URI scheme was set to `ws://` on line 32.

Setting `context=None` correctly resolves this.

(Before you ask why I'm using HTTP instead of HTTPS, I have the bot running within an overlay network under Rancher in AWS, so it communicates locally to Mattermost. If I provide the HTTPS URL for mattermost, it will exit the VPC and connect to the public IP for the load balancer, generating unnecessary usage charges. I terminate SSL on the load balancer, so I need internal communication to be unencrypted.)

issue_title: Suggestion: allow single extension in ng-accept
DjangoInputObjectType doesn't allow overwriting model fields
issue_body:
# Problem
I'm using custom logic for input validation of a model field (like, validate it as a string and then convert it into UUID v.5 before save). So, I have a model and schema defined below:

```
# models.py
class TestModel(models.Model):
    test_field = models.UUIDField()

# schema.py
class TestModelInput(DjangoInputObjectType):
    test_field = graphene.String(required=True)

    class Meta:
        model = TestModel

class CreateTestModel(graphene.Mutation):

    class Input:
        data = TestModelInput()
    
    @staticmethod
    def mutate(root, info, data):
        # here I convert and save my model

```
## What I expect from printing graphql schema:
```
input TestModelInput {
  test_field: String!
  ...
```
## What I get:
```
input TestModelInput {
  test_field: UUID!
  ...
```
# Solution
I found out that in the `graphene_django_extras/types.py:182` this line
```
super(InputObjectType, cls).__init_subclass_with_meta__(_meta=_meta, **options)
```
means that the method `InputObjectType.__init_subclass_with_meta__` itself is not being called and this class attributes are not being converted into fields. The line should be changed into
```
super(DjangoInputObjectType, cls).__init_subclass_with_meta__(_meta=_meta, **options)
```
to run correctly.

P.S. Thanks for the great package! It saved me lots of work =)

issue_body:
I'm trying to get a writable nested serializer working in Swagger.
This is my code:

models.py
```python
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import User

class Profile(models.Model):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User)
    points = models.IntegerField()

```
serializers.py
```python
from rest_framework import serializers
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from models import Profile

class ProfileSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    class Meta:
        model = Profile
        fields = ('points',)

class UserSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    profile = ProfileSerializer(required=False)
    
    def create(self, validated_data):
        profile_data = validated_data.pop('profile', None)
        user = super(UserSerializer, self).create(validated_data)
        self.create_or_update_profile(user, profile_data)
        return user

    def update(self, instance, validated_data):
        profile_data = validated_data.pop('profile', None)
        self.create_or_update_profile(instance, profile_data)
        return super(UserSerializer, self).update(instance, validated_data)

    def create_or_update_profile(self, user, profile_data):
        profile, created = Profile.objects.get_or_create(user=user, defaults=profile_data)
        if not created and profile_data is not None:
            super(UserSerializer, self).update(profile, profile_data)        

    class Meta:
        model = User
        fields = ('username','first_name', 'last_name','email','profile',)       

```
views.py

from rest_framework import viewsets

issue_title: How to support application/json request accept header
issue_body:
Why is this important? Well it seems the javascript swagger client will always set the accept header to 'application/json`: https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-js/issues/1124

When the client does the request against the schema url, it responds with:
`{"detail":"Could not satisfy the request Accept header."}`

Since there isn't an apparent resolution on the javascript side, I wanted to pipe `application/json` to the `OpenAPIRenderer` ; Sadly this results in some non-dry practices as the internals to get_swagger_view
are not extendable.

Ideally this application should work out of the box with `swagger-js` but this does not seem to be the case. At the very least there should be a documented path to get the two to work together.

Is there any plan to support the spatial transformer layer?

As of now, the only way to train a model with sparse operators is using the Module API (sparse support for Gluon API not ready yet). However, the existing Module API is not a good fit for training sparse models with KVStores present. This thread discusses how to provide a user-friendly module API for sparse symbols, which serves as a guideline before the feature is added into Gluon.

## Why the current Module API is not a good fit
Currently, if a module has KVStore present, it performs the following steps for each batch:
```python
for batch in batches:
    mod.forward(batch)
    mod.backward(batch)
    mod.update() which calls the following kvstore APIs
        # kvstore.push(grad) which aggregates & applies the gradients from the current batch
        # kvstore.pull(weight) which broadcast updated weights to all devices
```
This is not ideal for models w/ sparse operators because it is not necessary to broadcast full weights. Instead, only a subset of the weights are useful for the computation when data is sparse. 

Here is an example:
Let's say input `X` is has 100,000 features, where each sample only has around 10 non-zeros features. If the model computes `dot(X, W)`, not all rows in `W` are used for the computation. In fact, if `W` only contains the rows corresponding to the non-zero features in `X`, the result of `dot` is exactly the same compared to a `dot(X, W)` w/ full(dense) `W`. 

Avoiding the communication of these full(dense) weight/gradient is essential when one tries to scale the training over multiple machines and multiple devices. 

Therefore, we want to re-order the loop and do something like the following:
```python
for batch in batches:
    kvstore.row_sparse_pull(sparse_weight, useful_rows_for_this_batch) 
    kvstore.pull(dense_weights)
    mod.forward(batch)
    mod.backward(batch) 
    mod.update()
        # kvstore.push(grad) 
```
With the module API, one must access private members of module such as `_exec_group` and `_params_dirty` and interact with kvstore directly to achieve the above like this [example](https://github.com/eric-haibin-lin/mxnet/blob/lstmp/example/sparse/linear_classification/train.py#L97-L98). We need a module API that abstracts these away and make it more user-friendly. 

## contrib.SparseModule API
Modifying the existing module API to support this may over complicate the code and make it hard to maintain. On the other hand, a new SparseModule API is a chance to educate MXNet users who already have some experience with the Module API. I propose the following SparseModule API: 

### class SparseModule(Module)

A Module that wraps a network with sparse symbols.
The module provides abstraction for data parallel distributed training with "row sparse" weights,
supporting two types of communications: update_on_kvstore=True and update_on_kvstore=False.
Distributed training includes both multi-device and multi-machine training.

- update_on_kvstore=True
    Full parameters are stored in KVStore.
    For each data batch, only broadcast the subset of "row sparse" weights that are required for the computation,
    according to the row ids provided.
    Usually the row ids correspond to features that appear in the batch.
    
    Specifically, for each batch of data,
    sparse weights are broadcast to all devices/machines based on row ids.
    Each device/machine then computes forward/backward with sparse weights.
    Finally, gradients are reduced from all devices/machines and applied on the parameters stored in KVStore.


- update_on_kvstore=False
    Full parameters are stored and duplicated on each machine/device.
    For each data batch, compute forward/backward with full weights.
    Then gradients are then broadcast to all other devices/machines.
    Each device/machine applies the gradient to the copy of the parameters it holds.

Take the following linear network as an example:
```python
 data = mx.sym.var('data', stype='csr')
 label = mx.sym.var('label')
 weight = mx.sym.var('w0', shape=(1000,1), stype='row_sparse')
 bias = mx.sym.var('b0', shape=(1,))
 pred = mx.sym.broadcast_add(mx.sym.sparse.dot(data, weight), bias)
 output = mx.sym.LogisticRegressionOutput(pred, label)
 ```

 - update_on_kvstore=True
 ```python
 mod = mx.module.contrib.SparseModule(..., update_on_kvstore=True)
 for batch in batches:
     row_id_generator = lambda x: {'w0': x.data[0].indices}
     **mod.prepare(batch, row_id_generator=row_id_generator)**
     mod.forward(batch)
     mod.backward()
     mod.update()
 ```

 - update_on_kvstore=False
 ```python
 mod = mx.module.contrib.SparseModule(..., update_on_kvstore=False)
 for batch in batches:
     **mod.prepare(batch)**
     mod.forward(batch)
     mod.backward()
     mod.update()
 ```

Which type is recommended? So far only `update_on_kvstore=True` is implemented.
- Memory: `update_on_kvstore=False` in theory leads to higher memory consumption,
   because each device keeps a complete copy of the model.
   For extremely large models that don't fit on a single machine/device,
   keeping a complete copy of the model is not feasible.
- Computation: depends on the operator. For some operators,
   they benefit from the fact that weights are sparse (such as square_sum in factorization machine)
   and do less computation during forward.
   For other operators it might not be the case
   because sparse weight requires extra efforts for lookup/search(such as SparseEmbedding).

#### __init__(..., **update_on_kvstore=None**)
    update_on_kvstore : bool 
        Specifies whether to store a copy of the parameter and perform updates on the KVStore. 
        The value type could be extended to "bool or dict" in the future,
        if we want to support different behavior per parameter, 
        where the keys of the dictionary are the names of parameters, the values are boolean.
        e.g. {'weight_0':False, 'weight_1':True}.

#### prepare(batch, row_id_generator=None)
    Prepare the module for the current data batch. 
    update_on_kvstore=True, their updated values are pulled from the KVStore,
    based on the row ids generated by row_id_generator.

    row_id_generator: a lambda function which takes data_batch as the input, 
          and outputs a dictionary of str->NDArray. The keys in the dict are sparse param names,
          the values are the row_ids to pull for each param.

    Note that the reason why row_id_generator is a function is that users can customize what row ids to provide. It could be the `indices` field from a CSRNDArray, or the input data itself (such as user_id, movie_id). 

#### update
    Update parameters based on the gradients computed by the previous forward/backward.
    If update_on_kvstore=True, it updates the parameters stored in KVStore.
    Otherwise it updates the params stored locally on each device. 

#### init_optimizer
    initialize KVStore and optimizer. 
    There is no guarantee that the parameters on devices are in sync with the ones in KVStore. 
    The sync happens in `prepare()` instead. 

#### save_checkpoint
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull sparse weights before saving the checkpoint.

#### score
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull sparse weights before calling forward. 

#### iter_predict
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull sparse weights before calling forward. 

#### predict
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull sparse weights before calling forward. 

#### fit
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull sparse weights before calling forward. 

#### get_params
    Gets a copy of current parameters.
    If update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull all sparse weights. 

#### save_params
    if update_on_kvstore=True, need to pull all sparse weights. 

#### forward_backward

#### @property data_names
#### @property output_names
#### @property data_shapes
#### @property label_shapes
#### output_shapes
#### init_params
#### set_params

#### load_params
#### get_states
#### set_states
#### install_monitor

#### forward
#### backward

#### get_input_grads
#### update_metric
#### bind

#### @property symbol
#### @staticmethod load

issue_title: How to dynamically bind executor with variable symbol?
issue_body:
During testing phase, my symbol's computing structure depends on input shape. E.g, when input shape is K, then there is N(K) layers of Convolution (for example), where N(K) changes with K.
Thus, I have to construct a new symbol and rebind it with an executor when K changes. Code may look like this:
```
if current_k != last_k:
    sym = get_symbol(current_k)
    mod = mx.mod.Module(sym, ...)
    mod.bind(...)
else:
    # keep the module unchanged
    pass
```
My question is, how to effectively release memory allocated by the bound module and rebind it with a different symbol?
I've tried using python's 'del' to delete the bound module, but the memory is not released when look into the info returned by 'nvidia-smi'.

issue_title: recommend to use 0 as default value for mxnet.rnn.BucketSentenceIter's variable invalid_label instead of -1
issue_body:
In file [mxnet/python/mxnet/rnn/io.py](https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/blob/master/python/mxnet/rnn/io.py#L86)
line 86, the default value of invalid label is defined -1
however, if we use this default value, [cudnn_lstm_bucketing.py](https://github.com/dmlc/mxnet/blob/master/example/rnn/cudnn_lstm_bucketing.py) cannot run.

refers to the document of [mxnet.symbol.Embedding](http://mxnet.io/api/python/symbol.html#mxnet.symbol.Embedding) which requires all the input values should be integers in the range [0, input_dim).

setting the default value of invalid_label to be -1 is very dangerous since many nlp applications apply RNN and mxnet.symbol.Embedding layer together
I recommend to use 0 as default value

While possible to derive a span's timestamp and duration at query time, it is best to report them to zipkin. This reduces the guesswork on the server side, and particularly makes the cassandra storage implementation more effective. This also makes the duration query operable.

### Do we need to do anything?
Here's the normal doc on that zipkin.io/pages/instrumenting.html This elaborates timestamp and duration a bit, particularly that they can be supplied independently of annotations (sometimes more accurately that way, as a stopwatch isn't at risk of NTP updates)

Then, we'd need to ensure that the special-case of [continuing a client-originated span on the server](https://github.com/openzipkin/openzipkin.github.io/issues/49) is addressed (where B3 headers are read). This might happen here or in pyramid, depending on if we introduce B3 extraction here or not.

Here are tests that should be in place to ensure that span.timestamp and duration are set properly.

* client creates a span and propagates it to a server
  * client propagates b3 headers for trace id, span id, and sampled=1
  * server neither reports span.timestamp nor duration
  * client reports span.timestamp and duration

* server creates a new trace
  * no b3 headers are detected
  * server reports span.timestamp and duration

* server creates a new trace with externally supplied ids
  * caller propagates b3 headers for trace id, span id, but *no sampled header*
  * server reports span.timestamp and duration

Does this make sense? Can anyone here help implement this?

See https://github.com/openzipkin/brave/issues/277#issuecomment-262429392

```
if not obj_type and 'allOf' in schema_object_spec:
```
This should probably be `or`

## Issues

* When using `--import <baseline_filename>`, a baseline is created without knowledge of the existing baseline file. This means that the current baseline file will be scanned for secrets (which it will clearly find, due to the secret hashes stored in there).

* When we upgrade baselines, we currently need to perform a two-liner (without `sponge`):

```
$ detect-secrets scan --import .secrets.baseline > .secrets.baseline.new
$ mv .secrets.baseline.new .secrets.baseline
```

If we already know the filename we're importing from (as compared to reading from stdin), we should also write to it.

## Suggested Fix

```
$ detect-secrets scan --upgrade .secrets.baseline
```

This will write results to the provided file, and ignore the false positives in the current baseline file.

issue_title: checkstyle does not allow class scoped variables for staticmethod
issue_body:
It may be good idea to discuss this as a ticket.

Checkstyle prevents the use of class scoped variables with a staticmethod. 

```
class MyClass(object):

  VARIABLE = None

  @staticmethod
  def method():
    return MyClass.VARIABLE
```

$ check.pex

```
T800:WARNING science/src/python/twitter/packer/lib/packer_client.py:046 Instead of MyClass.VARIABLE use self.VARIABLE or cls.VARIABLE with instancemethods and classmethods respectively.
     |    return MyClass.VARIABLE
```

It is entirely desirable to have a) staticmethod  b) have a class scoped variables in staticmethod. We do not care for subclassing in staticmethods, so enforcing the users to change it to classmethods may not be a good idea.

issue_title: Various little errors leftover from commit 84c030a
issue_body: In the merge of commit 84c030a introduced several small typos and errors.

I'm following this tutorial : https://discourse.mozilla.org/t/tutorial-how-i-trained-a-specific-french-model-to-control-my-robot/22830 to create a French model. 

The problem is when generating the trie file with this command : 

> ./generate_trie data/cassia/alphabet.txt data/cassia/lm.binary data/cassia/vocabulary.txt data/cassia/trie

I have this output : 

> terminate called after throwing an instance of 'lm::FormatLoadException'
  what():  native_client/kenlm/lm/binary_format.cc:131 in void lm::ngram::MatchCheck(lm::ngram::ModelType, unsigned int, const lm::ngram::Parameters&) threw FormatLoadException.
The binary file was built for probing hash tables but the inference code is trying to load trie with quantization and array-compressed pointers
Abandon (core dumped)

I tried several times to generate my lm.binary with kenlm (./build_binary -T -s words.arpa  lm.binary) but still the same error.

As noticed in https://github.com/mozilla/mozmill-ci/issues/828 we have a kinda strict limitation to our regex for the binary of candidate and release builds:

https://github.com/mozilla/mozdownload/blob/master/mozdownload/scraper.py#L601

The problem here is that we are not able to download some of the files when using `--extension` because there is a different file naming convention for Windows and MacOS:

https://archive.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/candidates/52.0b2-candidates/build1/win32/en-US/
https://archive.mozilla.org/pub/firefox/candidates/52.0b2-candidates/build1/mac/en-US/

issue_title: SERVER_NAME differs from wsgiref when using an /etc/hosts entry
issue_body:
Hello,

Consider the following code : 
```python
import wsgiref.simple_server as wsgiref
import pulsar.apps.wsgi as wsgi  
                                                                               
def server_name(environ, start_response):                                                                                                      
    start_response('200 OK', [('Content-Type','text/plain; charset=utf-8')])                                                                   
    return [environ['SERVER_NAME'].encode('utf-8')]
                                               
if __name__ == '__main__':                                                                                                                     
    # wsgi.WSGIServer(callable=server_name).start()                                                                                            
    wsgiref.make_server('0.0.0.0', 8000, server_name).serve_forever()
```

If this runs on a remote server, for which I have an entry "server" in my /etc/hosts and I use : 

 `wget -qO- http://server:8000`

It returns the remote server hostname ( as returned by gethostname() )

When using Pulsar's WSGIServer (with --bind 0.0.0.0:8000) it returns 'server', probably because socket.getfqdn('server') fails in server.py and return its argument (and it takes a lot of time to do so because it triggers a DNS lookup, which is problematic in my setup).

I have not taken the time to dive further in the server.py code yet to make a pull request, but I will unless the fix is obvious to you.

Thanks !

issue_title: Clarification needed: multiget - calling queue.task_done without ever calling queue.join() [JIRA: CLIENTS-1083]
issue_body:
As seen in the [documentation](https://docs.python.org/2/library/queue.html#Queue.Queue.task_done), task_done is only used/cared for when you might be doing a Queue.join() call (looking at the python source code confirms this also). Which is never seen within multi.py, however I do see multiple instances of the task_done call; [example](https://github.com/basho/riak-python-client/blob/f13f0df8a566f0002210cb76bb942e9db8c25ea4/riak/client/multi.py#L222).

Is there some other undocumented side-affect of calling task_done in each of these places? Or is this likely an artefact of some older feature/ port from another language?

I realise that calling task_done doesn't cause anything to break, and keeps the queue in a state with a "more correct" view of what tasks it has processed, but i'm wondering if it _is_ used for something unexpected.
