issue_body: 我生成了一个动态图表，在以html为格式时可交互也可以动态，但是输出成gif或者svg就不能动态和交互，而且README中的svg示例已经挂了...

# Amadeus SDK Specification Document

### Requirement Prioritization

The following document follows the [MoSCoW](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/MoSCoW_method) method of prioritising rules. Please follow the following guidelines when evaluating rules.

* `MUST` - Rules labeled as __must__ are requirements that should not be deviated from at any cost
* `SHOULD` - Rules labeled as __should__ are requirements that could be deviated from if needed, though this will have to be documented and cleared with all stakeholders before it can be disregarded.
* `COULD` - Rules labeled as __could__ are requirements that are desirable but not necessary and therefore would be nice to have where time and resources permit.

We do not use the fourth __`won't`__ level in this specification.

## Limitations

Currently this specification is limited due to various reasons. This means that currently:

* This specification mostly focusses on read-only API methods
* This specification assumes a language that has objects, or object-like features
* This specification does not assume the SDK is automatically generated

## Maintenance Requirements

### 1. Source Control

- [x] __1.1__ The source code for the SDK __must__ be maintained within Git version control
- [x] __1.2__ Development of new features __should__ happen on feature branches
- [ ] __1.3__ Feature branches __should__ pass all tests and linting before they can be merged into the `master` branch
- [ ] __1.4__ Source control __should__ contain tags for each release of the SDK
- [x] __1.5__ The `master` branch __should__ be kept in a condition that allows for direct use through checkout
- [x] __1.6__ The source code __must__ be hosted publicly
- [x] __1.7__ The source code __should__ use GitHub for public hosting
- [x] __1.8__ The source code __should__ not include build packages, compiled assets, or any other intermediary files used to package the source code into a release

### 2. Releases & Versioning

- [x] __2.1__ The SDK __must__ use [Semantic Versioning](http://semver.org/) to increment the version number as changes are made
- [x] __2.2__ The version number of the SDK __could__ be incremented when the SDK has gathered enough changes to warrant a new release
- [ ] __2.3__ For every release a tag __must__ created within Git
- [ ] __2.4__ New releases __should__ be deployed automatically to the package manager using the CI server
- [ ] __2.5__ For every new release the `CHANGELOG` file __must__ to be updated with the `Major`, `Minor` and `Patch` changes
- [ ] __2.6__ Releases __must__ be pushed to package managers as an __Amadeus__ user not exclusively under any personal accounts
- [x] __2.7__ The version number of the SDK __should__ be independent of the API version
- [x] __2.8__ A release package __should__ not include unnecessary source code files or intermediarry files for the SDK.
- [x] __2.9__ A release package __must__ include the documentation `README` file
- [x] __2.10__ A release package __must__ include the `LICENSE` file
- [x] __2.11__ A release package __should__ include the `CHANGELOG` file
- [x] __2.12__ The name of the SDK __should__ follow language best practices, and be one of `amadeus`, `Amadeus`, or `amadeus/amadeus`.
- [x] __2.13__ If the preferred name of the SDK is not available, it __could__ be one of `amadeus-sdk`, `AmadeusSDK`, or `amadeusdev/amadeus`.
- [x] __2.14__ The name of the SDK __must__ exclude the programming language (e.g. __not__ `amadeus-php`) or a reference to this being an SDK client library (e.g. __not__ `amadeus-sdk`)
practices, and be one of `amadeus`, `Amadeus`, or `amadeus/amadeus`.
- [x] __2.15__ As soon as the first public version of the library has been signed off, the version __should__ be bumped to `1.0.0`


### 3. CI Server

- [x] __3.1__ A Continuous Integration (CI) server __must__ be used to automatically test any branch of the Git repository
- [x] __3.2__ The CI server __should__ be [Travis CI](https://travis-ci.org/).
- [x] __3.3__ The CI server __must__ test against all current LTS language versions
- [x] __3.4__ The CI server __could__ test against popular non-LTS versions
- [ ] __3.5__ The CI server __could__ test on different platforms, including Windows, Linux, and macOS.
- [ ] __3.6__ The CI server __should__ test new Git tags, and build and push the package to the package manager

## Additional Content Requirements

### 4. Documentation

- [x] __4.1__ The SDKs __must__ include a `README` file
    - [x] __4.1.1__ The `README` file __should__ include a version badge
    - [x] __4.1.2__ The `README` file __should__ include a test status badge
    - [x] __4.1.3__ The `README` file __must__ link to the `LICENSE` file
    - [x] __4.1.4__ The `README` file __should__ be written in Markdown
    - [x] __4.1.5__ The `README` file __must__ have instructions on how to install the SDK using a package manager
    - [ ] __4.1.6__ The `README` file __could__ have instructions on how to install the SDK from version control
    - [x] __4.1.7__ The `README` file __must__ have instructions on how to initialise the SDK with the API credentials
        - [x] __4.1.7.1__ Where possible, the initialised client variable __should__ be named `amadeus`. For example, `amadeus = new Amadeus::Client()`
    - [x] __4.1.8__ The `README` file __should__ document all the different ways the SDK can be initialized
    - [x] __4.1.9__ The `README` file __must__ include a basic sample on how to make a first API call
    - [ ] __4.1.10__ The `README` file __must__ link to the developer portal
    - [x] __4.1.11__ The `README` file __should__ link to documentation on the developer portal
    - [x] __4.1.12__ The `README` file __must__ document any installation requirements and prerequisites
    - [ ] __4.1.13__ The `README` file __should__ to official support channels
    - [x] __4.1.14__ The `README` file __must__ document where a developer can find their API credentials
- [x] __4.2__ The SDKs __should__ have its public methods documented in a way that allows for autogenerated method documentation. For example, for Ruby this would be [yard](https://yardoc.org/) and for Java this would be using Javadoc.
- [x] __4.3__ The SDKs __could__ use the CI server or any other build tool to autogenerate the method documentation on deploy
- [x] __4.4__ The GitHub repository __should__ have a title in the format "Ruby library for the Amadeus travel APIs"
- [x] __4.5__ The GitHub repository __should__ have the following tags: `amadeus`, `travel`, `flights`, `hotels`, `sdk`, `library`
- [x] __4.6__ The SDKs __must__ include a `CHANGELOG` file
- [x] __4.7__ The SDKs __must__ include a `CODE_OF_CONDUCT` file
- [x] __4.8__ The SDKs __must__ include a `CONTRIBUTING` file
    - [x] __4.8.1__ The Contribution Guidelines __should__ include instructions on how to run the SDK in development/testing mode.
- [x] __4.9__ The SDKs __must__ include a `ISSUE_TEMPLATE` file
- [x] __4.10__ The SDKs __must__ include a `PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE` file
- [x] __4.11__ The SDKs __must__ include a `SUPPORT` file

> Templates for a lot of these files have been provided in the [templates](./templates) folder

### 5. Testing

- [x] __5.1__ The SDKs __must__ be thoroughly tested
- [x] __5.2__ The tests __should__ have integration tests to make the API calls
- [x] __5.3__ The tests __should__ test response objects
- [x] __5.4__ The tests __must__ not actually make any HTTP calls to the API in testing and instead use some kind of VCR method
- [x] __5.5__ The tests __must__ not include actual API credentials

### 6. Linting

- [x] __6.1__ The SDKs __must__ have their files linted
- [x] __6.2__ The linting __must__ ensure that tabs/spaces are consistently used
- [x] __6.3__ The linting __should__ ensure no trailing whitespace is left in the code
- [x] __6.4__ The linting __should__ ensure quotes and brackets are consistently applied
- [x] __6.5__ The linting __could__ ensure semicolons are present when needed
- [x] __6.6__ The linting __could__ ensure comments are present on public methods

## Dependencies & Infrastructure Requirements

### 7. Dependencies

- [x] __7.1__ The SDK __must__ limit its runtime dependencies
- [x] __7.2__ The SDK __should__ have no runtime dependencies
- [x] __7.3__ The SDK __could__ use any amount of development and test dependencies

### 8. HTTP Client

- [x] __8.1__ The SDK __must__ use a well supported HTTP client
- [x] __8.2__ A HTTP client from the standard libraries __should__ be used
- [x] __8.3__ The HTTP __should__ support proxies
- [x] __8.4__ The SDK __could__ allow a developer to provide an alternative HTTP client

### 9. Logging

- [x] __9.1__ The SDK __must__ be able to log activities to a logger
- [x] __9.2__ The logger __should__ use the default runtime log
- [x] __9.3__ The logger __must__ allow enabling/disabling of debug mode per instance
- [x] __9.4__ The logger __should__ allow a developer to provide an alternative logger
- [x] __9.5__ When debugging is enabled, the logger __should__ log (and only log) the request object, response object, and optionally any raw HTTP response object of no response object could be formed.

### 10. Reporting

- [x] __10.1__ The SDK __must__ identify requests to the API as originating from the SDK
- [x] __10.2__ The SDK __must__ report the SDK version number to the API
- [x] __10.3__ The SDK __should__ report the language version number to the API
- [x] __10.4__ The HTTP client __should__ use the following format user agent to identify the library:
    - Specification: `library_name/library_version language_name/language_version`
    - Example with known language version: `amadeus-ruby/1.0.0 ruby/2.4.2`
- [x] __10.5__ The SDK __should__ allow a developer to provide an additional __custom app id__ and __custom app version__ to be passed along in the user agent.
    - Specification: `library_name/library_version language_name/language_version app_name/app_version`
    - Example: `amadeus-ruby/1.0.0 ruby/2.4.2 test_ios_app/1.0.0`

## Initialization & Interaction Requirements

### 11. Initialization

- [x] __11.1__ The SDK __could__ be included or imported when needed, and where applicable __must__ use the `amadeus`, `com.amadeus.developer`, or `Amadeus` package name
- [x] __11.2__ The SDK __should__ reside in its own namespace and avoid polluting the global namespace
- [x] __11.3__ The actual client object for the SDK __should__ exist as a subclass within the Amadeus namespace where the language allows. For for example `Amadeus::Client` in Ruby, or `Amadeus\Client` in PHP
- [x] __11.4__ The SDK client __must__ allow for the creation of multiple clients per runtime environment, allowing the creation of multiple clients with different credentials
- [x] __11.5__ The SDK client __must__ be able to accept SDK credentials as method parameters
- [x] __11.6__ The SDK client __should__ accept the SDK credentials implicitly as environment variables `AMADEUS_CLIENT_ID` and `AMADEUS_CLIENT_SECRET`
- [x] __11.7__ The SDK client __must__ accept a parameter to turn set the logger level
- [x] __11.8__ The SDK client __should__ be able to implicitly accept the debug level as environment variable `AMADEUS_DEBUG`
- [x] __11.9__ The SDK client __should__ be able to accept an alternative logger object
- [x] __11.10__ The SDK client __could__ accept an alternative HTTP client
- [x] __11.11__ The SDK client __must__ allow selection of the base URL by name (`test` and `production`)
- [x] __11.12__ The SDK client __must__ allow for setting a custom base URL directly

### 12. Namespacing

- [x] __12.1__ The SDK __should__ use namespaced methods to create a match between the API and the SDK
    - `GET /v1/flights` : `amadeus.flights.get`
    - `GET /v2/hotels/offers`: `amadeus.hotels.offers.get`
- [x] __12.2__ The SDK __could__ use resource IDs as parameters to the namespace, or as a name or unnamed parameter to the call used to execute the API call
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123` : `amadeus.hotels(123)`
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123` : `amadeus.hotels.get(123)`
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123` : `amadeus.hotels.get(id: 123)`
- [x] __12.3__ The SDK __should__ limit API calls when selecting sub resources. All of these should make 1 API call only
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123/hotel-offers` : `amadeus.hotels(123).offers`
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123/hotel-offers` : `amadeus.hotels.get(123).offers`
    - `GET /v1/hotels/123/hotel-offers` : `amadeus.hotels.get(id: 123).offers`
- [x] __12.4__ The SDK __could__ drop the HTTP verb methods where lazy loading is possible
    - `GET /v1/flights/123/legs` : `amadeus.flights(123).legs()` (should make 1 API call only)
    - `GET /v1/flights/123/legs/345` : `amadeus.flights(123).legs(345)` (should make 1 API call only)
- [x] __12.5-__ The SDK __could__ convert the API namespace (e.g. `reference-data`) to a more idiomatic format (e.g. `reference_data` or `ReferenceData`)

### 13. Method Syntax

- [x] __13.1__ The SDK API calls __should__ allow for a method to fetch all records for a resource with or without any parameters
    - Example: `amadeus.flights().get()` : `GET /v1/flights`
    - Example: `amadeus.flights().get({ foo: 123 }` : `GET /v1/flights?foo=123`
- [x] __13.2__ The SDK API calls __should__ allow for a method to take a record ID to fetch a specific resource
    - Example: `amadeus.flights(123).get()` : `GET /v1/flights/123`
- [x] __13.3__ When making a POST or PUT request, the SDK API calls __should__ accept a key/value data structure for the data to be submitted
    - Example: `amadeus.flights().post({ from: "LHR", to: "LAX" })`
- [x] __13.4__ In asynchronous programming languages, the SDK API calls __should__ allow for a callback method to be provided, or a promise to be returned
    - Example with callback `amadeus.flights().get(params, callback_function)`
    - Example with promise `amadeus.flights().get(params).then(...).catch(...)`
- [x] __13.5__ The SDK API calls __should__ return a response object

### 14. Error Handling

- [x] __14.1__ The SDK __should__ raise a `ResponseError` for any request that did not result a HTTP 200 or 201 response code
- [x] __14.2__ The SDK __should__ differentiate between different errors
    - [x] __14.2.1__ The SDK __should__ raise a `NetworkError` when a transport error occurs (e.g. if the API could not be reached, or unexpected data was returned from the API)
    - [x] __14.2.2__ The SDK __should__ raise a `AuthenticationError` when incorrect credentials were provided
    - [x] __14.2.3__ The SDK __should__ raise a `NotFoundError` when the API call returned a 404
    - [x] __14.2.4__ The SDK __should__ raise a `ClientError` when a generic client error occurs (e.g. incorrect data was sent to the API, basically any 4XXX error)
    - [x] __14.2.5__ The SDK __should__ raise a `ServerError` if the API returned a 5XX error]
    - [x] __14.2.6__ The SDK __should__ raise a `ParserError` if the response was JSON but could not be parsed
- [x] __14.3__ The response error object __must__ contain a reference to the response object, which itself has a reference to the request object that made the call
- [x] __14.4__ The response error object __should__ contain a `code` attribute containing the name of the error, e.g. `NetworkError`.
- [x] __14.5__ The response error object __should__ contain a `description` attribute containing the parsed JSON from the API, if the API errored and contained JSON.
- [x] __14.6__ The SDK __should__ not validate submitted data within the SDK, instead leaving this task to the API and raising exceptions on receiving an error response from the API

## API Mapping Requirements

### 15. API Calls

- [x] __15.1__ The SDK __should__ define the `reference-data` namespace
    - [x] __15.1.1__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v2/reference_data/urls/checkin-links`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/31#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.reference_data.urls.checkin_links.get({ airline: 'BA' })`
    - [x] __15.1.2__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v2/reference_data/locations`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/32#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.reference_data.locations.get({ keyword: 'LON' })`
    - [x] __15.1.3__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v2/reference_data/locations/airports`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/33#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.reference_data.locations.airports.get({ latitude: '0.0', longitude: '0.0' })`
- [x] __15.2__ The SDK __should__ define the `shopping` namespace
    - [x] __15.2.1__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/flight-destinations`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/35#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.flight_destinations.get({ origin: 'LAX' })`
    - [x] __15.2.2__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/flight-offers`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/36#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.flight_offers.get({ origin: 'LAX', destination: 'LHR', departureDate: '2020-12-01' })`
    - [x] __15.2.3__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/flight-dates`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/37#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.flight_dates.get({ origin: 'LAX', destination: 'LHR' })`
- [x] __15.3__ The SDK __should__ define the `travel/analytics` namespace
    - [x] __15.3.1__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/travel/analytics/fare-searches`](https://mobile-services.amadeus.com/catalogue/30#learn) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.travel.analytics.fare_searches.get({ origin: 'LAX', sourceCountry: 'US', period: 2015 })`
    - [x] __15.3.2__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/travel/analytics/air-traffics`](#) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.travel.analytics.air_traffics.get({ origin: 'LAX', period: '2015-07' })`
- [x] __15.4__ The SDK __should__ define the `shopping/hotels` namespace
    - [x] __15.4.1__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/hotel-offers`](#) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.hotel_offers.get({ cityCode: 'LAX' }`
    - [x] __15.4.2__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/hotels/:hotel_id/hotel-offers`](#) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.hotels.get(123).hotel_offers.get({ checkInDate: '2018-12-01', checkOutDate: '2018-12-03' })`
    - [x] __15.4.3__ The SDK __must__ implement the [`GET /v1/shopping/hotels/:hotel_id/offers/:offer_id`](#) endpoint
        - Example: `amadeus.shopping.hotels.get(123).hotel_offers.get(345)`

### 16. Responses

- [x] __16.1__ The SDK __must__ return a response object where possible, instead of the JSON data directly
- [x] __16.2__ The response object __must__ contain a `result` attribute with the parsed JSON content if the content could be parsed
- [x] __16.4__ The response object __must__ contain a `data` attribute with the content from the data key from the `result` hash, if it is present
- [x] __16.5__ The response object __must__ be parsed as well when an error occurred
- [x] __16.6__ An error __must__ be thrown if the response was JSON but could not be parsed    
- [x] __16.7__ The response object __must__ contain a `statusCode` attribute with HTTP status code of the response
- [x] __16.8__ The response object __must__ contain a `request` attribute with the details of the original request made
- [x] __16.9__ The response object __should__ contain a `parsed` attribute which should be `true` when the JSON was successfully parsed
- [x] __16.10__ The response object __should__ be able to deal with any new parameters returned from the API without needing an SDK update. In other words, the class definition of response objects should not define the attributes of the object statically
- [x] __16.11__ The response object __should__ remain lightweight and not contain any instance variables besides those needed to track what the API returned, keeping the logged output of the instance to the important details of the API call.

### 17. Requests

- [x] __17.1__ The SDK __must__ keep track of the details of a request in a request object that's accessible through the response object
- [x] __17.2__ The request object __must__ track the host, port, verb, path, params, bearerToken, headers (including User Agent) used for the call
- [x] __17.3__ The request object __should__ remain lightweight and not contain any instance variables besides those needed to track what API call was made, keeping the logged output of the instance to the important details of the API call.

### 18. Pagination

- [x] __18.1__ The SDK __must__ allow for easy pagination of responses
- [x] __18.2__ The SDK __must__ expose `.next`, `.first`, `.last`, `.previous` methods on the API client to find the relevant page for a response
  - Example given a previous response: `let next_response = amadeus.next(response);`
- [x] __18.3__ The SDK __should__ not expose any pagination function on the response objects, as this would require the response objects to keep a reference to the API client, limiting the ability to easily debug a response object

## Key Developer Experience Interactions

### 19. Successful Path Interactions

- [x] __19.1__ Finding a location by type __should__ allow for using a built in type
   - Ruby example: `locations = amadeus.reference_data.locations.get(keyword: 'lon', subType: Amadeus::Location::Airport)`
   - Node example: `let locations = amadeus.referenceData.locations.get({ keyword: 'lon', subType: Amadeus.location.airport });`
- [x] __19.2__ Making a query using a location code __should__ be able to use the response from a location query
   - Ruby example: `amadeus.foo.bar(origin: locations.data.first['iataCode'])`
   - Node example: `amadeus.foo.bar({ origin: locations.data.first.iataCode });`

### 20. Unsuccessful Path Interactions

- [x] __20.1__ When incorrect credentials are provided, the error returned __should__ be clear even when debug mode is off
<details>
<summary>Ruby example:</summary>
   
```ruby
amadeus.get('/foo/bar')
```

```ruby
W, [2018-02-19T16:06:29.881202 #67814]  WARN -- Amadeus AuthenticationError: {
  "error": "invalid_client",
  "error_description": "Client credentials are invalid",
  "code": 38187,
  "title": "Invalid parameters"
}
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Node example:</summary>
   
```js
amadeus.client.get('/foo/bar').then(...).catch(...);
```

```js
Amadeus AuthenticationError { error: 'invalid_client',
  error_description: 'Client credentials are invalid',
  code: 38187,
  title: 'Invalid parameters' }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Python example:</summary>
   
```js
amadeus.get('/foo/bar')
```

```js
Amadeus AuthenticationError: {'code': 38187,
 'error': 'invalid_client',
 'error_description': 'Client credentials are invalid',
 'title': 'Invalid parameters'}
 ```
</details><br/>

- [x] __20.2__ When an unknown path is provided, the error returned __should__ be clear even when debug mode is off
<details>
<summary>Ruby example:</summary>
   
```ruby
amadeus.get('/foo/bar')
```

```ruby
W, [2018-02-19T16:06:13.523516 #67786]  WARN -- Amadeus NotFoundError: [
  {
    "code": 38196,
    "title": "Resource not found",
    "detail": "The targeted resource doesn't exist",
    "status": 404
  }
]
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Node example:</summary>
   
```js
amadeus.client.get('/foo/bar').then(...).catch(...);
```

```js
Amadeus NotFoundError [ { code: 38196,
    title: 'Resource not found',
    detail: 'The targeted resource doesn\'t exist',
    status: 404 } ]
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Python example:</summary>
   
```python
amadeus.get('/foo/bar')
```

```python
Amadeus NotFoundError: [{'code': 38196,
  'detail': "The targeted resource doesn't exist",
  'status': 404,
  'title': 'Resource not found'}]
```
</details><br/>

- [x] __20.3__ When incorrect params are provided, the error returned __should__ be clear even when debug mode is off
<details>
<summary>Ruby example:</summary>
   
```ruby
amadeus.reference_data.locations.get(
  subType: Amadeus::Location::ANY
)
```

```js
W, [2018-02-19T16:05:55.923870 #67772]  WARN -- Amadeus ClientError: [
  {
    "status": 400,
    "code": 32171,
    "title": "MANDATORY DATA MISSING",
    "detail": "Missing mandatory query parameter",
    "source": {

      "parameter": "keyword"
    }
  }
]
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Node example:</summary>
   
```js
amadeus.referenceData.locations.get({
  keyword: 'lon'
}).then(...).catch(...);
```

```js
Amadeus ClientError [ { status: 400,
    code: 32171,
    title: 'MANDATORY DATA MISSING',
    detail: 'Missing mandatory query parameter',
    source: { parameter: 'subType' } } ]
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>Ruby example:</summary>
   
```python
amadeus.reference_data.locations.get(
  subType: Amadeus::Location::ANY
```

issue_title: provide a structured output for the validation
issue_body:
Right now the validators must return the status and a message, we want something more structurized:

status, message, details

where details is a list of errors the validator found. each error is a dict containing those keys:

- domain": "global",
- reason: a string describing the reason of the failure ("required", etc)
- message: a strng detailing the problem
- locationType : where the faulty parameter is to be found in the request ("header", "body", "path", "method")
- location:  The actual parameter name

The response will be a json object containing all this information
